response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack credentials, distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s with low-latency D code), and genuine geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as primary languages, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments a decade ago (2014-2015) with no Rust. Most recent work is TypeScript/Node.js, which is a poor match for a routing tiling pipeline role. The geospatial background and systems-level thinking (DLang async I/O, fibers, caching) make this a borderline 'maybe' rather than a clear 'no'.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map collaboration
Distributed low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-perf DBs)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, workshops on web internals
concerns: No modern C++ experience since 2015 and no Rust experience at all
Last decade dominated by TypeScript/Node.js/web stack, not systems programming
No demonstrated experience with routing algorithms, map tiling, or navigation pipelines
